attacking_style: Long-range opportunist who attacks 30-50km from finish on varied terrain, including unfamiliar climbs and flat breakaway stages. Capable of solo efforts requiring sustained power output rather than explosive accelerations.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support in finale positioning; relies on personal initiative rather than organized lead-outs. Team coordination issues evident in technical finales where positioning critical.
terrain_preference: Excels on descents and flat-to-rolling breakaway stages requiring diesel engine efforts. Shows surprising climbing competence on medium-length ascents (Signal de Uchon-type climbs), but not pure mountain stages.
vulnerability: Poor positioning in technical sprint finales; tactical passivity when aggressive descending could break races open. Appears to undercook natural advantages waiting for teammates rather than imposing tempo.
key_patterns: Wins from long breakaways (30-50km) requiring sustained solo efforts, not late accelerations (2 instances)
Demonstrates tactical hesitancy on descents despite superior skills, waits passively rather than attacking (2 instances)
Shows versatility beyond classics role: climbing improvement, stage hunting across terrain types (3 instances)
Positioning errors in technical finales undermine sprint opportunities, team miscommunication evident (2 instances)
Morale-driven performances: attacks with confidence after team setbacks to restore momentum (2 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2021 showed winning capability but tactical restraint limited potential; descending advantage underutilized suggesting mental approach issue rather than physical decline
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 16
